Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: create a separate exemption regime for activities involving the use of a controlled substance or precursor that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: specify the purposes for which an exemption may be granted for those activities; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: set out the information that must be submitted to the Minister of Health before the Minister may consider an application for an exemption in relation to a supervised consumption site.
Whereas Parliament recognizes that the objectives of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act (“the Act”) are the protection of public health and the protection of public safety;

Whereas the Act and its regulations have a dual role of prohibiting certain activities associated with harmful substances and allowing access to those substances for legitimate medical, scientific and industrial purposes;

Whereas the diversion of controlled substances and precursors, as those terms are defined in the Act, which are frequently used in the production of illicit drugs, is a worldwide problem with significant impacts on Canada;

Whereas the money that is used to purchase controlled substances that are obtained from illicit sources often originates from criminal activity such as theft, and that money, in turn, often funds organized crime in our communities;

Whereas the substances that are subject to the Act may pose serious risks to the health of individuals and those risks are exacerbated when those substances are unregulated, untested and obtained from illicit sources;
Whereas the negative consequences associated with the use of illicit substances can have significant impacts on vulnerable subsets of the Canadian population;

And whereas an exemption from the application of the Act and its regulations for certain activities in relation to controlled substances that are obtained from illicit sources should only be granted in exceptional circumstances and after the applicant has addressed rigorous criteria;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Respect for Communities Act.
Subsection 30(2) of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An inspector shall be provided with a prescribed certificate of designation and, on entering any place under subsection 31(1) or a supervised consumption site under subsection 31(1.1) or (1.2), shall, on request, produce the certificate to the person in charge of the place.
Section 31 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Powers of inspectors — inspection of supervised consumption sites
Subsection.Text: In order to confirm any information in relation to an application submitted to the Minister for an exemption for a medical purpose under subsection 56.1(2) to allow certain activities to take place at a supervised consumption site, as defined in subsection 56.1(1), an inspector may, at any reasonable time, enter the site and may for that purpose exercise any of the powers set out in paragraphs (1)(a) to (i).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Powers of inspectors — inspection of supervised consumption sites
Subsection.Text: In order to verify compliance or to prevent non-compliance with the provisions of this Act or the regulations, or with any terms and conditions specified by the Minister in an exemption granted under subsection 56.1(2), an inspector may, at any reasonable time, enter any supervised consumption site, as defined in subsection 56.1(1), and may for those purposes exercise any of the powers set out in paragraphs (1)(a) to (i).
Subsection 31(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
In the case of a dwelling-place, an inspector may enter it only with the consent of an occupant or under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (3).
Paragraph 31(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a place referred to in subsection (1) or (1.2) is a dwelling-place but otherwise meets the conditions for entry described in that subsection,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 31(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

The owner or other person in charge of a place entered by an inspector under subsection (1), (1.1) or (1.2) and every person found there shall give the inspector all reasonable assistance in that person’s power and provide the inspector with any information that the inspector may reasonably require.
Subsection 31(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an inspector determines that to ensure compliance with the regulations or with any terms and conditions specified by the Minister in an exemption granted under subsection 56.1(2) it is no longer necessary to detain a controlled substance or a precursor seized by the inspector under paragraph (1)(i), the inspector shall notify in writing the owner or other person in charge of the place where the seizure occurred of that determination and, on being issued a receipt for it, shall return the controlled substance or precursor to that person.
Paragraph 55(1)(n) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the qualifications for inspectors and their powers and duties in relation to the enforcement of, and compliance with, the regulations or any terms and conditions specified by the Minister in an exemption granted under subsection 56.1(2);

Subsection.Text: Section 55 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
A regulation made under paragraph (1)(z) shall not exempt from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any person or class of persons in relation to a controlled substance or precursor that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any controlled substance or precursor or any class of either of them that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes of section 56.1, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: defining terms for the purposes of that section;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: amending the definitions that are set out in subsection 56.1(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting any information to be submitted to the Minister under paragraph 56.1(3)(z.1) and the manner in which it is to be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an exemption may be granted for a medical or law enforcement purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting requirements in relation to an application for an exemption made under subsection 56.1(2), including the information to be submitted with the application; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting terms and conditions in relation to an exemption granted under subsection 56.1(2).

Section.Text: Section 56 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on any terms and conditions that the Minister considers necessary, exempt from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations any person or class of persons or any controlled substance or precursor or any class of either of them if, in the opinion of the Minister, the exemption is necessary for a medical or scientific purpose or is otherwise in the public interest.
The Minister is not authorized under subsection (1) to exempt from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any person or class of persons in relation to a controlled substance or precursor that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any controlled substance or precursor or any class of either of them that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: alternate person in charge means any person designated by the applicant who is responsible, when the responsible person in charge is absent from the supervised consumption site, for ensuring that every person or class of persons who is exempted for a medical purpose under subsection (2) from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations complies with any terms and conditions specified by the Minister in the exemption when they are at the site.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence involving the financing of terrorism against any of sections 83.02 to 83.04 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence involving fraud against any of sections 380 to 382 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the offence of laundering proceeds of crime against section 462.31 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an offence involving a criminal organization against any of sections 467.11 to 467.13 of the Criminal Code; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, being an accessory after the fact in relation to, or any counselling in relation to, an offence referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (d).

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: infraction désignée en matière de drogue

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against section 39, 44.2, 44.3, 48, 50.2 or 50.3 of the Food and Drugs Act, as those provisions read immediately before May 14, 1997;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against section 4, 5, 6, 19.1 or 19.2 of the Narcotic Control Act, as those provisions read immediately before May 14, 1997;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under Part I of this Act, except subsection 4(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, being an accessory after the fact in relation to, or any counselling in relation to, an offence referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (c).

Definition.Text: illicit substance means a controlled substance that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act.

key staff members means the persons designated by the applicant who are responsible for the direct supervision, at the supervised consumption site, of the consumption of an illicit substance by every person or class of persons who is exempted for a medical purpose under subsection (2) from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a council of an incorporated city, metropolitan area, town, village or other municipality;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an authority responsible for delivering municipal services to an unincorporated city, metropolitan area, town, village or other municipality;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a council of the band, as that term is defined in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a government of a band that is a party to a comprehensive self-government agreement that is given effect by an Act of Parliament.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the geographical area of an incorporated or unincorporated city, metropolitan area, town, village or other municipality;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reserve and designated lands, as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: lands that are subject to a comprehensive self-government agreement that is given effect by an Act of Parliament.

Definition.Text: responsible person in charge means the person, designated by the applicant, who is responsible, when the person is at the supervised consumption site, for ensuring that every person or class of persons who is exempted for a medical purpose under subsection (2) from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations complies with the terms and conditions specified by the Minister in the exemption when they are at the site.

supervised consumption site means a location specified in the terms and conditions of an exemption, granted by the Minister under subsection (2) for a medical purpose, that allows any person or class of persons described in the exemption to engage in certain activities in relation to an illicit substance within a supervised and controlled environment.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on any terms and conditions that the Minister considers necessary, exempt from the application of all or any of the provisions of this Act or the regulations, other than this section, if, in the opinion of the Minister, the exemption is necessary for a medical, law enforcement or prescribed purpose

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any person or class of persons in relation to a controlled substance or precursor that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any controlled substance or precursor or any class of either of them that is obtained in a manner not authorized under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may consider an application for an exemption for a medical purpose under subsection (2) that would allow certain activities to take place at a supervised consumption site only after the following have been submitted:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: scientific evidence demonstrating that there is a medical benefit to individual or public health associated with access to activities undertaken at supervised consumption sites;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a letter from the provincial minister who is responsible for health in the province in which the site would be located that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: outlines his or her opinion on the proposed activities at the site,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: describes how those activities are integrated within the provincial health care system, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: provides information about access to drug treatment services, if any, that are available in the province for persons who would use the site;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a letter from the local government of the municipality in which the site would be located that outlines its opinion on the proposed activities at the site, including any concerns with respect to public health or safety;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a description by the applicant of the measures that have been taken or will be taken to address any relevant concerns outlined in the letter referred to in paragraph (c);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a letter from the head of the police force that is responsible for providing policing services to the municipality in which the site would be located that outlines his or her opinion on the proposed activities at the site, including any concerns with respect to public safety and security;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a description by the applicant of the proposed measures, if any, to address any relevant concerns outlined in the letter referred to in paragraph (e);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a letter from the lead health professional, in relation to public health, of the government of the province in which the site would be located that outlines their opinion on the proposed activities at the site;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a letter from the provincial minister responsible for public safety in the province in which the site would be located that outlines his or her opinion on the proposed activities at the site;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the potential impacts of the proposed activities at the site on public safety, including the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: information, if any, on crime and public nuisance in the vicinity of the site and information on crime and public nuisance in the municipalities in which supervised consumption sites are located,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: information, if any, on the public consumption of illicit substances in the vicinity of the site and information on the public consumption of illicit substances in the municipalities in which supervised consumption sites are located, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: information, if any, on the presence of inappropriately discarded drug-related litter in the vicinity of the site and information on the presence of inappropriately discarded drug-related litter in the municipalities in which supervised consumption sites are located;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: law enforcement research or statistics, if any, in relation to the information required under subparagraphs (i)(i) to (iii);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: relevant information, including trends, if any, on the number of persons who consume illicit substances in the vicinity of the site and in the municipality in which the site would be located;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: relevant information, including trends, if any, on the number of persons with infectious diseases that may be in relation to the consumption of illicit substances in the vicinity of the site and in the municipality in which the site would be located;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: relevant information, including trends, if any, on the number of deaths, if any, due to overdose — in relation to activities that would take place at the site — that have occurred in the vicinity of the site and in the municipality in which the site would be located;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: official reports, if any, relevant to the establishment of a supervised consumption site, including any coroner’s reports;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: a report of the consultations held with the professional licensing authorities for physicians and for nurses for the province in which the site would be located that contains each authority’s opinion on the proposed activities at the site;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: a report of the consultations held with a broad range of community groups from the municipality in which the site would be located that includes

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a summary of the opinions of those groups on the proposed activities at the site,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: copies of all written submissions received, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the steps that will be taken to address any relevant concerns that were raised during the consultations;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: a financing plan that demonstrates the feasibility and sustainability of operating the site;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the drug treatment services available at the site, if any, for persons who would use the site and the information that would be made available to those persons in relation to drug treatment services available elsewhere;

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: relevant information, including trends, on loitering in a public place that may be related to certain activities involving illicit substances, on trafficking of controlled substances and on minor offence rates in the vicinity of the site, if any;

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: information on any public health emergency in the vicinity of the site or in the municipality in which the site would be located that may be in relation to activities involving illicit substances as declared by a competent authority with respect to public health, if any;

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the measures that will be taken to minimize the diversion of controlled substances or precursors and the risks to the health and the safety and security of persons at the site, or in the vicinity of the site, including staff members, which measures must include the establishment of procedures

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to dispose of controlled substances, precursors, and any thing that facilitates their consumption, including how to transfer them to a police officer,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to control access to the site, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: to prevent the loss or theft of controlled substances and precursors;

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: a description of record keeping procedures for the disposal, loss, theft and transfer of controlled substances and precursors — and any thing that facilitates their consumption — left at the site;

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: the name, title and resumé, including relevant education and training, of the proposed responsible person in charge, of each of their proposed alternate responsible persons, and of each of the other proposed key staff members;

Paragraph.Label: (x)
Paragraph.Text: a document issued by a Canadian police force in relation to each person referred to in paragraph (w), stating whether, in the 10 years before the day on which the application is made, in respect of a designated drug offence or a designated criminal offence, the person was

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: convicted as a young person in ordinary court, as those terms were defined in subsection 2(1) of the Young Offenders Act, chapter Y-1 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1985, immediately before that Act was repealed, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a young person who received an adult sentence, as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Youth Criminal Justice Act;

Paragraph.Label: (y)
Paragraph.Text: if any of the persons referred to in paragraph (w) has ordinarily resided in a country other than Canada in the 10 years before the day on which the application is made, a document issued by a police force of that country stating whether in that period that person

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was convicted as an adult for an offence committed in that country that, if committed in Canada, would have constituted a designated drug offence or a designated criminal offence, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: received a sentence — for an offence they committed in that country when they were at least 14 years old but less than 18 years old that, if committed in Canada, would have constituted a designated drug offence or a designated criminal offence — that was longer than the maximum youth sentence that could have been imposed under the Youth Criminal Justice Act for such an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (z)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that the Minister considers relevant to the consideration of the application; and

Paragraph.Label: (z.1)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed information that is submitted in the prescribed manner.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may consider an application for an exemption for a medical purpose under subsection (2) that would allow certain activities to continue to take place at an existing supervised consumption site only after, in addition to the information referred to in paragraphs (3)(a) to (z.1), the following have been submitted:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: evidence, if any, of any variation in crime rates in the vicinity of the site during the period beginning on the day on which the first exemption was granted under subsection (2) in relation to the site and ending on the day on which the application is submitted; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: evidence, if any, of any impacts of the activities at the site on individual or public health during that period.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may only grant an exemption for a medical purpose under subsection (2) to allow certain activities to take place at a supervised consumption site in exceptional circumstances and after having considered the following principles:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: illicit substances may have serious health effects;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: adulterated controlled substances may pose health risks;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the risks of overdose are inherent to the use of certain illicit substances;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: strict controls are required, given the inherent health risks associated with controlled substances that may alter mental processes;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: organized crime profits from the use of illicit substances; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: criminal activity often results from the use of illicit substances.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may give notice of any application, in the form and manner determined by the Minister, for an exemption for a medical purpose under subsection (2) to allow certain activities to take place at a supervised consumption site. Members of the public have 90 days after the day on which the notice is given to provide the Minister with comments.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Act come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
